anchors.0: Absent or incorrect (does not encode the required semantics, or encodes the wrong semantics).
anchors.1: Attempted but incomplete (partial structure present, but missing a required link or contains invalid/broken references).
anchors.2: Correct (encodes the required semantics with valid references and usable structure for AT).

principle: Native HTML first; use ARIA only when native semantics do not express required relationships or announcements.

DOC_VALID_HTML.title: Valid HTML fragment and no forbidden content

decision_rules.2: Contains at least one HTML element tag and the HTML parser produces at least one element. Contains no markdown fences ('```' or '~~~') and contains no <script> or <style> elements.
decision_rules.1: Contains at least one HTML element tag and the HTML parser produces at least one element, but includes <script> or <style> elements when meta.forbid_script_style=false.
decision_rules.0: Contains any markdown fence ('```' or '~~~') or the HTML parser produces no elements, or (meta.forbid_script_style=true and contains <script> or <style>).

DOC_VALID_HTML.notes:
What it checks:
- The output is actual HTML that parses into elements.
- No markdown fences.
- If meta.forbid_script_style=true, rejects <script> and <style>.
Why it matters:
- Stops non-HTML or unsafe fragments from entering scoring.
Examples:
- 0: "```<input>```" or "Just explaining" (no HTML)
- 1: "<script></script><input id='a'>" when meta.forbid_script_style=false
- 2: "<label for='a'>Name</label><input id='a'>"

ID_UNIQUENESS.title: All id values are unique within the fragment

decision_rules.2: All id attribute values are unique in the fragment, or no ids exist.
decision_rules.1: At least one id value is duplicated, and none of the duplicated id values appear in any association reference set (label for=, aria-labelledby, aria-describedby, aria-errormessage).
decision_rules.0: At least one id value is duplicated, and a duplicated id value appears in an association reference set (label for=, aria-labelledby, aria-describedby, aria-errormessage).

ID_UNIQUENESS.notes:
What it checks: - Every id value appears at most once. - A duplicate is worse when label/help/error references use it. Why it matters: - Duplicate ids can make associations point to the wrong element. Example: - 0: "
" - 1: "
" - 2: "
"

REF_TARGET_EXISTS.title: All association references point to existing elements

decision_rules.2: Every id reference used by label for=, aria-labelledby, aria-describedby, and aria-errormessage points to an element that exists in the fragment. If aria-labelledby or aria-errormessage is used, referenced targets contribute non-empty text after trimming whitespace.
decision_rules.1: At least one reference list contains a mix of existing and missing targets, or aria-labelledby or aria-errormessage resolves but the referenced text is empty or whitespace-only.
decision_rules.0: All references point to ids that do not exist in the fragment.

REF_TARGET_EXISTS.notes:
What it checks:
- Every id mentioned by label for=, aria-labelledby, aria-describedby, aria-errormessage exists.
- aria-labelledby and aria-errormessage must point to real text.
Why it matters:
- Broken references remove the link between a control and its label/help/error.
Examples:
- 0: all referenced ids are missing
- 1: some referenced ids exist and some are missing, or referenced text is empty
- 2: all referenced ids exist and referenced text is non-empty

NAME_ROLE_VALUE_NATIVE.title: Correct native element for intended role and state

decision_rules.2: The primary control is a native form control element (input, select, textarea, or button). If the variant expects a specific native kind via meta.expected_primary_kind, it matches. If the variant expects file/radio/checkbox via meta flags, the required native input types are present. No role override is applied to the primary native control.
decision_rules.1: The primary control is native, but an essential attribute needed to express the requested semantics is missing or mismatched (such as missing input type when meta.input_type_required=true), or a role override is present on the native control, or the input type is present but not aligned with meta.expected_input_types when those are provided.
decision_rules.0: No native primary control exists, or the output uses non-native elements with ARIA roles as the main control, or the native element encodes the wrong control semantics for the expected variant (e.g., file expected but not input[type=file]).

NAME_ROLE_VALUE_NATIVE.notes:
What it checks: - The main interactive thing is a native control. - Avoids fake controls (div/span + role) as the main control. - Uses meta to enforce file/radio/checkbox expectations when needed. Why it matters: - Native controls expose stable semantics to browsers and AT. Example: - 0: "
" - 1: "" - 2: ""

LABEL_ASSOCIATION.title: Programmatic label association for primary control

decision_rules.2: A valid programmatic label association exists through one mechanism: (a) <label for=ID> where the primary control has id=ID; or (b) the primary control is inside a <label>; or (c) aria-labelledby on the primary control references at least one existing element and the concatenated referenced text is non-empty after trimming whitespace.
decision_rules.1: Label-related content exists but association is incomplete or fragile: label exists but for= is missing or broken; or aria-labelledby exists but referenced ids are missing; or aria-labelledby targets exist but contribute no text.
decision_rules.0: No programmatic label association exists for the primary control.

LABEL_ASSOCIATION.notes: What it checks: - The primary control is linked to a label via for=, label wrapping, or aria-labelledby with real text. Why it matters: - Without a programmatic link, AT may not announce the field name. Example: - 0: "Email" - 1: "" - 2: ""

LABEL_VISIBLE_OR_EQUIV.title: Label text is present and not replaced by placeholder

decision_rules.2: The primary control has non-empty label text from either <label> text or aria-labelledby referenced text after trimming whitespace. Placeholder may exist but is not the only naming source.
decision_rules.1: The primary control has a name only through aria-label (non-empty after trimming whitespace) with no non-empty label text from <label>/aria-labelledby.
decision_rules.0: No non-empty label text exists from <label>/aria-labelledby, and placeholder is the only apparent naming source, or all naming sources are empty/whitespace-only.

LABEL_VISIBLE_OR_EQUIV.notes:
What it checks:
- There is real label text (label/aria-labelledby), not just placeholder.
Why it matters:
- Placeholder-only naming disappears and is unreliable.
Example:
- 0: "<input id='e' placeholder='Email'>"
- 1: "<input id='e' aria-label='Email'>"
- 2: "<label for='e'>Email</label><input id='e' placeholder='name@example.com'>"

DESCRIPTION_ASSOCIATION.title: Help/description text is programmatically associated

decision_rules.2: meta.description_ids is a non-empty list, at least one element with id in meta.description_ids exists and has non-empty text, and the relevant target (primary control, or group container if the variant uses group-level association) has aria-describedby that includes at least one id from meta.description_ids and all referenced ids exist.
decision_rules.1: meta.description_ids is a non-empty list and aria-describedby exists on the relevant target, but at least one referenced id is missing, or aria-describedby does not include any id from meta.description_ids, or all meta.description_ids targets exist but have empty/whitespace-only text.
decision_rules.0: meta.description_ids is a non-empty list and aria-describedby is missing on the relevant target.

DESCRIPTION_ASSOCIATION.notes:
What it checks: - If description is expected, the description exists and the control/group links to it via aria-describedby. Why it matters: - Unlinked help text can be missed and not announced. Example: - 0: "
Use full name
" - 1: "
Use full name
" - 2: "
Use full name
"

REQUIRED_STATE.title: Required state encoded

decision_rules.2: meta.required_expected=true and the primary control encodes required via required attribute, or aria-required='true' when used without conflict.
decision_rules.1: meta.required_expected=true and required intent appears in text (e.g., 'required' or '*'), but the primary control has neither required nor aria-required='true', or required encoding is present but conflicting.
decision_rules.0: meta.required_expected=true and there is no required encoding and no required intent text.

REQUIRED_STATE.notes:
What it checks:
- If required is expected, required is encoded on the control.
Why it matters:
- AT can only detect required fields when encoded.
Example:
- 0: "<label for='n'>Name</label><input id='n'>"
- 1: "<label for='n'>Name (required)</label><input id='n'>"
- 2: "<label for='n'>Name</label><input id='n' required>"

REQUIRED_CUE_EXPLAINED.title: Required symbol cue is handled safely when present

decision_rules.2: meta.required_expected=true and either no '*' symbol is present, or '*' is present and is hidden from AT via aria-hidden='true'. If meta.required_symbol_explanation_required=true, the fragment also contains an explicit explanation mentioning 'required'.
decision_rules.1: meta.required_expected=true and '*' symbol is present but not hidden from AT, or meta.required_symbol_explanation_required=true but no explicit 'required' explanation text is present.
decision_rules.0: (Unused) Reserved for hard failures; kept for scale compatibility.

REQUIRED_CUE_EXPLAINED.notes: What it checks: - If an asterisk is used, it is hidden from AT. - Optional visible explanation when requested by meta. Why it matters: - Avoids asterisk being read as the only cue. Example: - 2: "" - 1: ""

AUTOCOMPLETE_PRESENT.title: Autocomplete attribute present when applicable

decision_rules.2: meta.expected_autocomplete_tokens is a non-empty list and the primary control has an autocomplete attribute with a non-empty value after trimming whitespace.
decision_rules.1: meta.expected_autocomplete_tokens is a non-empty list and autocomplete exists but is empty/whitespace-only, or is exactly 'on'/'off' (case-insensitive).
decision_rules.0: meta.expected_autocomplete_tokens is a non-empty list and the primary control has no autocomplete attribute.

AUTOCOMPLETE_PRESENT.notes:
What it checks:
- If expected tokens are provided, autocomplete is present and non-empty.
Why it matters:
- Helps faster, fewer-error form filling.
Example:
- 0: "<input id='e' type='email'>"
- 1: "<input id='e' autocomplete='on'>"
- 2: "<input id='e' autocomplete='email'>"

AUTOCOMPLETE_VALID_TOKEN.title: Autocomplete token is appropriate for field purpose

decision_rules.2: meta.expected_autocomplete_tokens is a non-empty list and the primary control has autocomplete exactly equal to one value in meta.expected_autocomplete_tokens.
decision_rules.1: meta.expected_autocomplete_tokens is a non-empty list and autocomplete exists but it is empty/whitespace-only or is exactly 'on'/'off' (case-insensitive).
decision_rules.0: meta.expected_autocomplete_tokens is a non-empty list and (a) the primary control has no autocomplete attribute, or (b) the primary control has a non-empty autocomplete value that is not in meta.expected_autocomplete_tokens.

AUTOCOMPLETE_VALID_TOKEN.notes:
What it checks:
- If expected tokens are provided, autocomplete matches one of them exactly.
Why it matters:
- Wrong tokens can trigger wrong autofill.
Example:
- 0: "<input id='e' type='email'>" (missing autocomplete)
- 0: "<input id='e' autocomplete='cc-number'>"
- 1: "<input id='e' autocomplete='on'>"
- 2: "<input id='e' autocomplete='email'>"

INPUT_TYPE_MATCHES_PURPOSE.title: Input type matches field purpose

decision_rules.2: meta.expected_input_types is a non-empty list and the primary control is an input whose type is one of the values in meta.expected_input_types (case-insensitive).
decision_rules.1: meta.expected_input_types is a non-empty list and the primary control is input type='text' while 'text' is not in meta.expected_input_types (treated as a generic fallback).
decision_rules.0: meta.expected_input_types is a non-empty list and the primary control is not an input, or the input type is a clear semantic mismatch (e.g., checkbox/radio/file/submit for a textlike field), or the input type is non-empty and not in meta.expected_input_types without being a reasonable fallback.

INPUT_TYPE_MATCHES_PURPOSE.notes:
What it checks:
- If expected input types are provided, the input type matches.
Why it matters:
- Type affects keyboard, validation, and meaning.
Example:
- 0: "<input id='e' type='password'>"
- 1: "<input id='e' type='text'>"
- 2: "<input id='e' type='email'>"

CONSTRAINT_PATTERN_EXPLAINED.title: Pattern/format constraint explained to user

decision_rules.2: meta.pattern_expected=true and the primary control has a pattern attribute, and meta.pattern_guidance_ids is a non-empty list, and at least one guidance element exists with non-empty text, and aria-describedby on the primary control references at least one id in meta.pattern_guidance_ids with all referenced ids existing.
decision_rules.1: meta.pattern_expected=true and the primary control has a pattern attribute, and meta.pattern_guidance_ids is non-empty, but aria-describedby is missing or does not include any id from meta.pattern_guidance_ids, or referenced ids are missing, or guidance text is empty/whitespace-only.
decision_rules.0: meta.pattern_expected=true and the primary control has no pattern attribute, or meta.pattern_guidance_ids is missing/empty, or no guidance element exists with non-empty text.

CONSTRAINT_PATTERN_EXPLAINED.notes:
What it checks: - If a pattern is expected, pattern exists and guidance text is linked. Why it matters: - Users need the format to avoid repeated errors. Example: - 0: "" - 1: "
5 digits
" - 2: "
5 digits
"

ERROR_PRESENT.title: Error message exists in text when requested

decision_rules.2: meta.error_ids is a non-empty list and at least one element with id in meta.error_ids exists and has non-empty text that is more specific than a generic word like 'Error'.
decision_rules.1: meta.error_ids is a non-empty list and at least one element with id in meta.error_ids exists but the text is empty/whitespace-only or overly generic (e.g., 'Error').
decision_rules.0: meta.error_ids is a non-empty list and no element with id in meta.error_ids exists.

ERROR_PRESENT.notes:
What it checks: - If error elements are expected, at least one exists and has meaningful text. Why it matters: - Vague or missing errors block correction. Example: - 0: "" - 1: "
Error
" - 2: "
Enter a valid email address.
"

ERROR_ASSOCIATED.title: Error message is programmatically associated to control/group

decision_rules.2: meta.error_ids is a non-empty list and the relevant target (primary control, or group container when meta.group_validation_expected=true) has aria-describedby or aria-errormessage that includes at least one id in meta.error_ids, and all referenced ids exist.
decision_rules.1: meta.error_ids is a non-empty list and aria-describedby or aria-errormessage exists on the relevant target, but at least one referenced id is missing, or none of the referenced ids are in meta.error_ids.
decision_rules.0: meta.error_ids is a non-empty list and there is no aria-describedby and no aria-errormessage on the relevant target.

ERROR_ASSOCIATED.notes:
What it checks: - If errors are expected, the control (or group) links to the error. Why it matters: - Error must be discoverable in context. Example: - 0: "
Bad email
" - 1: "
Bad email
" - 2: "
Bad email
"

ERROR_INVALID_STATE.title: Invalid state encoded

decision_rules.2: meta.error_ids is a non-empty list and aria-invalid='true' is set on the relevant target (primary control, or group container when meta.group_validation_expected=true).
decision_rules.1: meta.error_ids is a non-empty list and aria-invalid exists but is not exactly 'true' (case-insensitive), or aria-invalid='true' is placed on a non-relevant element while missing on the relevant target.
decision_rules.0: meta.error_ids is a non-empty list and aria-invalid is missing on the relevant target.

ERROR_INVALID_STATE.notes:
What it checks: - If an error is expected, aria-invalid='true' appears on the correct element. Why it matters: - Consistent invalid state for AT and tooling. Example: - 0: "
Bad email
" - 1: "
Bad email
" - 2: "
Bad email
"

STATUS_ANNOUNCE.title: Status/error announcements support AT when requested

decision_rules.2: meta.status_ids is a non-empty list, at least one element with id in meta.status_ids exists, and at least one such element has role='status' or role='alert' (case-insensitive) or has aria-live with a non-empty value after trimming whitespace.
decision_rules.1: meta.status_ids is a non-empty list, at least one element with id in meta.status_ids exists, but none has role='status'/'alert' and aria-live is missing or empty/whitespace-only.
decision_rules.0: meta.status_ids is a non-empty list and no element with id in meta.status_ids exists.

STATUS_ANNOUNCE.notes:
What it checks: - If a status region is expected, it exists and is marked as live. Why it matters: - Updates get announced to AT. Example: - 0: status missing - 1: status exists but not live - 2: "
Saved
"

GROUP_FIELDSET_LEGEND.title: Related controls grouped with fieldset/legend

decision_rules.2: meta.group_required=true and the fragment contains a fieldset with a legend whose text is non-empty after trimming whitespace.
decision_rules.1: meta.group_required=true and a fieldset exists but legend is missing/empty, or the fragment uses role='group' and the group container has a non-empty accessible name via aria-label or aria-labelledby with valid references.
decision_rules.0: meta.group_required=true and there is no fieldset and no usable role='group' fallback.

GROUP_FIELDSET_LEGEND.notes:
What it checks:
- For option sets, fieldset + legend is preferred.
Why it matters:
- Announces the group question/name.
Example:
- 0: radios with no group name
- 1: fieldset no legend, or named role=group
- 2: "<fieldset><legend>Choose a size</legend>..."

RADIO_SAME_NAME.title: Radio options share same name

decision_rules.2: meta.radio_group_expected=true and all radio inputs in the fragment share the same non-empty name value after trimming whitespace.
decision_rules.1: meta.radio_group_expected=true and at least two radios share a non-empty name, but at least one radio is missing name or has a different name.
decision_rules.0: meta.radio_group_expected=true and there is no consistent non-empty shared name across radios, or fewer than 2 radios exist.

RADIO_SAME_NAME.notes:
What it checks:
- For radio groups, all radios share one name.
Why it matters:
- Radios only act as single-choice with shared name.
Example:
- 0: missing names
- 1: mixed names
- 2: all name='size'

EACH_OPTION_LABELED.title: Each option has its own programmatic label

decision_rules.2: meta.option_inputs_expected=true and for every radio/checkbox input, there is a valid programmatic name source via label for=, label wrapping, aria-labelledby with existing targets and non-empty referenced text, or non-empty aria-label.
decision_rules.1: meta.option_inputs_expected=true and at least one option is labeled, but at least one option lacks a valid association or has broken references or empty label text.
decision_rules.0: meta.option_inputs_expected=true and no options have a valid label association, or no option inputs exist.

EACH_OPTION_LABELED.notes:
What it checks:
- Every option has its own name.
Why it matters:
- Unlabeled options are hard to use in AT.
Example:
- 0: radios with no labels
- 1: some labeled
- 2: each input has a label

DISABLED_STATE.title: Disabled state encoded correctly

decision_rules.2: meta.disabled_expected=true and the primary control has the disabled attribute present.
decision_rules.1: meta.disabled_expected=true and aria-disabled='true' is present on the primary control but disabled is missing, or disabled intent appears on some other element in the fragment (any element has disabled or aria-disabled='true') while the primary control lacks disabled.
decision_rules.0: meta.disabled_expected=true and no disabled encoding exists on the primary control and no other element in the fragment indicates disabled.

DISABLED_STATE.notes:
What it checks:
- If disabled is expected, the actual control is disabled.
Why it matters:
- Ensures true non-interaction and correct state exposure.

READONLY_STATE.title: Readonly state encoded correctly

decision_rules.2: meta.readonly_expected=true and the primary control has the readonly attribute present.
decision_rules.1: meta.readonly_expected=true and aria-readonly='true' is present but readonly is missing, or readonly intent appears on some other element in the fragment (any element has readonly or aria-readonly='true') while the primary control lacks readonly.
decision_rules.0: meta.readonly_expected=true and no readonly encoding exists on the primary control and no other element in the fragment indicates readonly.

READONLY_STATE.notes:
What it checks:
- If readonly is expected, readonly is set on the actual control.
Why it matters:
- Readonly stays focusable and must be consistent.

FILE_INPUT_SEMANTICS.title: File input uses correct native semantics

decision_rules.2: meta.file_input_expected=true and input[type=file] exists. Required attributes listed in meta.file_required_attributes are present. If meta.file_required_attributes includes 'accept', accept is non-empty and all accept tokens are well-formed. If meta.expected_accept_tokens is non-empty, accept covers those expected tokens at a high level (including allowance for broad tokens like image/* or application/pdf).
decision_rules.1: meta.file_input_expected=true and input[type=file] exists, but at least one required attribute in meta.file_required_attributes is missing or empty, or accept is required and empty/malformed, or accept is present and being evaluated against meta.expected_accept_tokens but does not cover the expected tokens at a high level.
decision_rules.0: meta.file_input_expected=true and input[type=file] is missing.

FILE_INPUT_SEMANTICS.notes:
What it checks:
- When a file upload is expected, uses input[type=file].
- Enforces any attributes listed in meta.file_required_attributes.
- If 'accept' is required, validates accept tokens and (when meta.expected_accept_tokens exists) checks coverage with broad-token allowances.
Why it matters:
- File inputs have built-in browser and AT behavior.
Examples:
- 0: file expected but no input[type=file]
- 1: input[type=file] exists but required attributes are missing/empty, or accept is malformed, or accept lacks expected coverage
- 2: input[type=file] exists and meets required attributes; accept (when required) is valid and covers expected types

BUTTON_TYPE_AND_NAME.title: Button has correct type and accessible name

decision_rules.2: meta.button_expected=true and either a <button> exists or an input[type=submit|reset|button] exists, it has a non-empty accessible name (button text, input value, or aria-label), and if meta.expected_button_type is set it matches.
decision_rules.1: meta.button_expected=true and a native button exists but the type mismatches expected_button_type, or the name relies only on aria-label (no visible text/value).
decision_rules.0: meta.button_expected=true and no native button exists, or the button has no accessible name.

BUTTON_TYPE_AND_NAME.notes:
What it checks:
- If a button is expected, it exists and has a usable name.
- Enforces expected type when declared.
Why it matters:
- Prevents unnamed buttons and type mismatches.

components.name: Email Field with Required, Autocomplete, and Accessible Description
Toggle Switch Group with Multiple Types

implementation_notes.meta_contract: Each component variant may supply component_meta to the scorer. Meta controls applicability and expected ids/types. If a check is not applicable for a variant, the scorer returns score=2 with a rationale like 'Not applicable; score=2 by rule.'
implementation_notes.aria_policy: ARIA checks are scored only when the component includes descriptions, errors, or status messages that require programmatic association or announcement. Native semantics are preferred; unnecessary ARIA can reduce score under NAME_ROLE_VALUE_NATIVE if it misstates role or state.
implementation_notes.why_not_all_need_aria: Many native form controls already expose role, state, and value to assistive tech. ARIA is mainly used to associate external descriptions or errors, or to announce status changes.
implementation_notes.scope_note: Behavioral checks are excluded because evaluation is restricted to HTML-only fragments without scripts or runtime interaction.
implementation_notes.meta_source_of_truth: Component semantics must be enforced via component_meta in the component variant definition (for example in components.json). Avoid relying on component name substring matching to infer expected_input_types or other semantics.

C17.reason: Quantity Field should enforce native input[type=number].

C27.reason: Confirmation Checkbox should enforce native input[type=checkbox].

C28.reason: Confirmation Checkbox with Link should enforce native input[type=checkbox].

C54.reason: Toggle Switch should be implemented as a native checkbox for stable semantics.

C55.reason: Toggle Switch with Accessible Description should be implemented as a native checkbox.

C56.reason: Toggle Switch Group should be treated as a group of native checkbox inputs and grouped with fieldset/legend or a named role=group fallback.

naming_conventions_for_meta.stable_component_intent: If a component semantic intent is not unambiguously derivable from its name, the variant must set component_meta explicitly, especially expected_input_types and input_type_required.
examples: Do not rely on 'Quantity Field' string matching; set expected_input_types=['number'].
Do not rely on 'Toggle Switch' string matching; set expected_input_types=['checkbox'].
Do not rely on 'Confirmation Checkbox' string matching; set expected_input_types=['checkbox'].
